initial_draft:

<my resources> ZUP: 100 </my resources>
<my goals> Buy resources with <ZUP>. You are willing to pay at most X: 60 ZUP for the resources. </my goals>
<reason> The initial offer is exactly at my maximum willingness to pay. I will accept this trade. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Sounds good, RED! I accept your offer of 1 X for 60 ZUP. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present and in the correct order. The content within each tag appears valid.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting at 60 ZUP is exactly at my maximum willingness to pay, so it maximizes my utility. No improvement possible.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent already proposed this trade, so acceptance is highly plausible.
4. Consistency: OK. The reason clearly states why I'm accepting (at my maximum valuation). The message reinforces this acceptance.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I’ve used only one proposal and am now accepting, respecting the proposal limit.
</critique>
